summary: High‑energy MSG set with volcanic Scarlet>Fire and soaring Terrapin
blurb: The show delivers a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue, a powerhouse Terrapin Station jam, and tight first‑set energy. Jerry’s guitar cuts through the mix while Phil supplies crushing bass under the jams.
review: On September 14, 1990 at Madison Square Garden the Dead turned in one of their most electrifying late‑‘80s/early‑’90s performances. The first set crackles with Feel Like A Stranger and Sugaree before building into a high‑octane run that includes New Minglewood Blues, Ramble On Rose, and Black Throated Wind. The second set erupts with the now‑legendary Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition, followed by a searing Truckin' > Terrapin Station sequence that showcases Jerry’s soaring solos and Phil’s “crushing” bass foundation. The Other One weaves into Wharf Rat and culminates in an unforgettable Sugar Magnolia, while the encore U.S. Blues caps a night that felt both tight and wildly improvisational. Reviewers consistently praise the crystal‑clear soundboard capture (especially gd1990-09-14.sbd.miller.74405.sbeok.flac16) for its balanced mix that lets every instrument shine, even as some note Vince Welnick’s keyboard tone still finding its footing.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Nine of ten analyzed recordings are positive, repeatedly highlighting the Scarlet>Fire sequence, Terrapin Station jam, and overall high‑energy performance. Only one mixed review notes keyboard issues; no consistent negative comments about playing. This strong consensus drives a large positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with clear, well‑balanced mix; highlights all key jams and preserves the band’s energy without dropouts.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue that ignites the second set
Terrapin Station extended jam with soaring guitar work
The Other One into Wharf Rat transition builds momentum toward a breathtaking close
First‑set high‑octane energy featuring Feel Like A Stranger and Sugaree

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar leads are front‑and‑center, delivering soaring solos on Scarlet and Terrapin; his vocal is strong early but shows slight strain later in the set.
band_performance.Phil: Provides crushing, prominent bass that drives the Fire jam and locks into every tempo shift with precision.
band_performance.Bob: Rocks a powerful opening vocal on Feel Like A Stranger, keeps rhythm tight, and rallies the crowd throughout.
band_performance.Vince: Keyboard tone is uneven—some critics call it awkward or unfinished—but he solidly supports Terrapin Station and The Other One sections.
band_performance.Mickey: Adds subtle percussive textures that complement the dual‑drum groove without overshadowing the main drive.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers volcanic, energetic drumming that propels Scarlet>Fire and Sugar Magnolia, giving the set its relentless momentum.
